title: SACRAMENTO -  Senator Dave Cortese (D-Silicon Valley) is commending the Cal Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), for citing & now investigating retaliation claims

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Nearly 50% of farmworkers continue to report that their farming employers are not complying with existing heat prevention laws and regulations according to a UC Merced study.",
    "The Ruiz Farm Labor investigation was launched following reports that the farm labor contractor turned a group of farmworkers away after they left their work shifts early during a heat wave.",
    "Senator Dave Cortese's Senate Bill 1299 aimed to establish the Farmworker Climate Change Heat Injury and Death Fund.",
    "Senate Bill 1299 would have promoted compliance with existing outdoor heat regulations through a rebuttable presumption for heat-related injury and death.",
    "Senate Bill 1299 aimed to ensure farmworkers who suffer a work-related heat injury, illness, or death would be treated and compensated expeditiously.",
    "The bill would not have created any additional workers’ compensation benefit level beyond what is otherwise available under existing workers’ compensation law."
  ]
}
```
